sources.notes: Statement and constraints: reflected motion inside a right isosceles triangular billiard.
Official editorial for Codeforces Round 1021; used to confirm the reflection-unfolding and congruence approach.
Luogu writeup cross-checks the unfolded-plane target condition and extended-gcd congruence solving.
Independent writeup cross-checks reducing boundary hits to counting intersections with unfolded grid/diagonal lines.

annotation.summary: Determine whether a point moving with reflections inside a right isosceles triangle ever reaches a vertex, and count boundary hits before escape.
annotation.constraints: Coordinates and velocities are up to 1e9 and t is large, so the solution must use number theory and O(log V) arithmetic rather than simulation.
annotation.core_idea: Unfold the reflected triangular billiard into a tessellated plane. The moving point then travels in a straight line with velocity (vx,vy), and escaping through a vertex is equivalent to first hitting a reflected vertex whose coordinates are multiples of n. This gives congruence equations x+t*vx ≡ 0 mod n and y+t*vy ≡ 0 mod n, solved by gcd/CRT or extended Euclid. Once the earliest positive hit time is known, count how many unfolded boundary lines the segment crosses: vertical and horizontal grid lines plus the two diagonal families corresponding to the hypotenuse reflections, taking care not to count the final vertex as a boundary hit.
tricks: Unfold reflections so the point moves in a straight line.
Solve simultaneous modular equations for the first reflected vertex hit.
Count crossed boundary-line families in the unfolded plane.

solution_variants.name: unfolded-crt-line-counting
solution_variants.summary: Solve for the first time the straight unfolded trajectory reaches lattice vertex coordinates, then count crossings with vertical, horizontal, and diagonal boundary families.

tags.evidence: The main reduction replaces reflected motion inside the triangle by straight-line motion through reflected copies of the triangle.
tags.description: Unfold a reflection or billiards process by reflecting the domain, turning a reflected path into a straight-line path in a tiled plane.
tags.created_reason: Generic geometry does not distinguish the standard billiards/reflection unfolding technique used as the decisive reduction here.

tags.evidence: Escaping requires solving simultaneous congruences for the time when both unfolded coordinates are multiples of n.
The boundary-hit count depends on geometric line families generated by reflections of the triangle sides.
The congruence system is checked and solved through gcd/extended Euclid divisibility conditions.
The solution has several boundary-counting and final-vertex off-by-one cases over large integer coordinates.
